title: TC001-Dashboard loads with core analytics widgets and charts visible (authenticated)
description: Verifies an authenticated user can open the dashboard and see key widgets/charts rendered.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /en/authentication to start the login flow.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Password' tab to switch to password sign-in flow and reveal email/password inputs.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type 'example@gmail.com' into the email field, type 'password123' into the password field, then click the Sign In button to submit credentials.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 1163) again to attempt login (2nd attempt), wait for the app to redirect/render the dashboard, then verify the dashboard URL and that 'Dashboard', 'Charts', and 'Trades' elements are visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator("xpath=//*[normalize-space(text())='Charts']").first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator("xpath=//*[normalize-space(text())='Trades']").first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Authentication did not redirect to '/dashboard' after submitting credentials.
- Current URL remains '/en/authentication' after two sign-in attempts.
- Dashboard content (heading 'Dashboard') is not visible on the page.
- Key dashboard widgets/elements ('Charts', 'Trades') are not present on the page.

title: TC002-Navigate from Dashboard to Strategies page using in-app navigation
description: Verifies user reaches Trade Desk (/dashboard/strategies) from authenticated dashboard navigation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to '/en/authentication' (explicit navigate as specified by test step).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the password auth tab (data-testid="auth-password-tab") to reveal the password sign-in fields.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type email into email input (index 1117), type password into password input (index 1121), then click the Sign In with Password button (index 1124).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Retry signing in by clicking the 'Sign In with Password' button again and wait for the SPA to redirect; then look for the dashboard nav item (data-testid='nav-trade-desk'). If nav-trade-desk appears, click it; if not, report the feature issue and finish the task.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard/strategies' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Dashboard navigation item with data-testid='nav-trade-desk' not found on the page after sign-in attempts
- Application did not redirect to dashboard; URL does not contain '/dashboard/strategies'
- Sign-in form remains visible with email and password populated after submit, indicating authentication did not complete
- Two sign-in attempts were performed and no error message or dashboard access appeared

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' link to open the authentication page (use element index 260).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/footer/div/div/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /en/authentication using the explicit navigate action (per test instruction).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Password' tab to switch to password sign-in flow and reveal the password input and submit button (index 1918).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type example@gmail.com into the email field (index 2108), type password123 into the password field (index 2112), then click the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 2115).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Attempt sign-in again by clicking 'Sign In with Password' (index 2115) and wait up to 3 seconds for the app to redirect to the dashboard. If the dashboard appears, proceed to locate and click the 'Reports' navigation item. If sign-in still fails, report the issue and mark the task done.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Net P&L').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"report-chart") or contains(@data-testid,"report-chart") or .//svg]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

- Dashboard navigation items ('Reports', 'Dashboard', 'Trade Desk', 'Billing', 'Settings', 'Data') are not present in the UI after sign-in attempts.
- The 'Reports' tab and 'Net P&L' metric cannot be accessed because the dashboard was not reached.
- No visible error message explains the sign-in failure, so the cause of the failure is unknown.

title: TC009-Discover pricing from home and reach authentication via pricing CTA
description: Verifies a visitor can navigate from the home page to pricing, view plan details, and use a pricing CTA to reach the authentication page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click on the link labeled "Pricing" (interactive element index 131).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/header/div/div/nav/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Attempt the 'Pricing' nav click again by clicking element 131. If it still does not navigate, look for alternative navigation elements (e.g., a 'Pricing' link or 'Compare Plans' anchor) before using direct navigation.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/header/div/div/nav/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Scroll the page to ensure plans section is reachable, then click the visible pricing CTA 'Start basic' (element index 2266) to navigate to the authentication page (/en/authentication).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/div/div/section/div/div/div/div/div/div[3]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/en' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Pricing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/en/pricing' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Plan').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- ASSERTION: Pricing CTA ('Start basic') did not navigate to /en/authentication when clicked from /en/pricing.
- ASSERTION: Clicking the 'Pricing' nav link (element 131) did not consistently navigate on click (two click attempts logged as failed) despite /en/pricing being reachable.
- ASSERTION: Authentication page was reached via the 'Sign In' link instead of the pricing CTA, so the expected CTA flow is not implemented.
- ASSERTION: Browser state reported 0 interactive elements on the authentication page after navigation in the page model, indicating possible rendering/selector issues affecting CTA behavior.

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability

            ],
        )

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

title: TC014-Login via /en/authentication password flow and land on dashboard

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode

        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /en/authentication to start the authentication flow test.

        # -> Click the 'Password' tab to reveal the password sign-in form (click element index 788).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Enter the email into the password form (index 1006) and then enter the password (index 1010), then click the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 1013). After submitting, verify the dashboard redirect and Dashboard text visibility.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/en/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:

title: TC015-Open billing page from dashboard shell after authentication
description: Verifies the billing route is reachable and renders billing/subscription content for an authenticated user.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to '/en/authentication' using the navigate action (explicit test step requires direct navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Password' tab to reveal the password sign-in fields (use element index 814).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type 'example@gmail.com' into the email field (index 1011), then type 'password123' into the password field (index 1015), then click the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 1018).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to '/en/dashboard/billing' (explicit test step requires direct navigation).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/dashboard/billing", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/en/dashboard/billing' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Billing').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Billing page did not render after navigation; authentication page displayed with a 'next' query parameter instead.
- Current URL is 'http://localhost:3001/en/authentication?next=%2Fen%2Fdashboard%2Fbilling' instead of '/en/dashboard/billing'.
- Text 'Billing' is not visible on the page.
- Login attempt (1/1) did not produce an authenticated session or redirect to the dashboard.

title: TC004-Open a dashboard widget and view its detailed panel/state
description: Verifies a dashboard widget can be opened and its details are visible to the user.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' link (index 260) to open the authentication page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/footer/div/div/div/div[2]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Password' tab (index 1995) to switch to password login so email and password inputs (auth-email-input/auth-password-input) and the submit button become available.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type the email into the email input (index 2223) then type the password into the password input (index 2227) and submit by clicking Sign In (index 2230).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Attempt to submit the password sign-in again by clicking the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 2230) to trigger authentication and then wait for the dashboard to render.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the cookie banner 'Accept All' button (index 2156) to remove the overlay, then wait for the UI to update so authentication can complete or error messages appear.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div/div/div/div[2]/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Trades').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Trade table').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Close').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Authentication did not complete after two 'Sign In with Password' attempts; the page remained on /en/authentication with the email and password fields visible.
- Dashboard/navigation elements (e.g., nav-trade-desk or nav-dashboard) did not appear after sign-in, preventing access to the dashboard.
- Trades widget could not be located or opened because the dashboard was not reachable.
- No explicit error message indicating invalid credentials or a network error was displayed, so the cause of the failed sign-in is unclear.

title: TC006-Navigate across Dashboard, Reports, and back to Dashboard without losing UI shell
description: Verifies authenticated navigation between dashboard and reports using stable nav selectors.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to '/en/authentication' to locate the password auth tab and perform login using data-testid selectors.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click on the password auth tab (data-testid="auth-password-tab") to reveal the password input and submit controls.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type '{{LOGIN_USER}}' into the email input (data-testid='auth-email-input', index 1006), then type '{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}' into the password input (data-testid='auth-password-input', index 1010), then click the Sign In with Password button (index 1013).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('{{LOGIN_USER}}')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Replace placeholder credentials with test credentials and submit the password form so authentication can proceed (fill email with example@gmail.com and password with password123, then click Sign In with Password).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In with Password' button (index 1013) to submit credentials and start authentication.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard/reports' in frame.url
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Dashboard navigation items with data-testid 'nav-reports' and 'nav-dashboard' were not found on the page after submitting credentials.
- The authentication page remained on /en/authentication: the sign-in form (email input id=email_password and password input id=password_login) and the 'Sign In with Password' button were still visible after submission.
- The 'Sign In with Password' button was clicked twice with no navigation to '/dashboard' or '/dashboard/reports' observed.
- Valid credentials were entered (email 'example@gmail.com' and password 'password123') yet the application did not redirect to dashboard routes.

title: TC008-Authentication required messaging when attempting dashboard access (in-session unauthenticated)
description: Verifies protected dashboard access redirects unauthenticated sessions to the authentication flow.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en/dashboard (use navigate action as this test step explicitly requires).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/dashboard", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/en/authentication' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[@data-testid="auth-password-tab"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC011-Navigate from home to support page from the public header/navigation
description: Verifies a visitor can open the Support page and see contact/help content.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the link labeled 'Support' in the top navigation to open the Support page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/header/div/div/nav/div[5]/a').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Assert we are on the /en page
        assert "/en" in frame.url
        # Wait briefly for navigation to finish after the click
        await page.wait_for_timeout(1000)
        # Assert the Support page is loaded (URL contains /en/support)
        assert "/en/support" in frame.url
        # Verify the 'Support' link/text is visible (header link)
        support_link = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/header/div/div/nav/div[5]/a')
        assert await support_link.is_visible()
        # Verify support contact/help content is visible (support message textarea)
        support_textarea = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[3]/div[2]/div/div[2]/div/div/div/div/section/form/div[1]/textarea')
        assert await support_textarea.is_visible()
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC016-Open settings page and verify account settings surface

import asyncio
from playwright import async_api

        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to '/en/dashboard/settings' (explicit navigation per test plan) so the page can be loaded and assertions performed.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/dashboard/settings", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Password' tab (index 3032) to reveal the password inputs so the sign-in flow can proceed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type the email into index 3210, type the password into index 3214, click Sign In with Password (index 3217), then navigate to /en/dashboard/settings.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/en/dashboard/settings' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Settings').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC017-Open import page and verify trade import entrypoint is visible
description: Verifies import entrypoint is accessible through the dashboard import trigger and opens modal flow.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001/en
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /en/authentication (use navigate action to the exact path)
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/en/authentication", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the password authentication tab (element index 765) to reveal the password inputs.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div/button[2]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type example@gmail.com into the email input (element index 969), then enter password123 into the password input (element index 973), then click the Sign In with Password button (element index 976).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div[2]/main/div[2]/div/div/section[2]/div/div[3]/div/div[3]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//*[@data-testid="dashboard-import-trigger"]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Import Data').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
